title: Digital Realty (DLR) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Digital Realty (DLR) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 4.5% FCF growth rate balances the explosive demand for data center capacity against the massive, ongoing capital expenditures required to build and equip new facilities. While revenue is growing steadily, free cash flow expansion is tempered by this intense capital intensity.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.8% discount rate reflects the higher cost of debt for the REIT and the risks associated with an elevated debt-to-equity ratio of 0.93. The reliable, long-term nature of its leases provides a partial offset to this financial risk.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 2.5% aligns closely with long-term global GDP and inflation expectations, assuming data centers remain the fundamental bedrock of the digital economy indefinitely.

thesisTitle: The Physical Layer of the Digital Economy
thesisParagraphs: Digital Realty operates as the critical physical infrastructure supporting the modern digital economy. Its expansive, carrier-neutral facilities globally provide the necessary power, cooling, and interconnection for hyperscalers and enterprises. The rapid emergence of generative AI has fundamentally altered the demand curve, ensuring virtually every megawatt of new capacity is leased well before construction is completed.

Despite this phenomenal demand profile, investors must recognize the capital-intensive nature of the business. The company must continually issue debt and equity to fund multibillion-dollar development pipelines, leading to projected near-term EPS headwinds (-9.97% next 5Y). At a current price near $180, the market appears to fairly price both the massive secular tailwinds and the heavy capital burden required to capture them.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 4.5% FCF growth rate for DLR?
faq.answer: While top-line revenue is growing strongly, the massive capital expenditures required to build AI-ready data centers significantly drags on free cash flow generation. The 4.5% rate reflects this balance.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for DLR's DCF?
faq.answer: An 8.8% discount rate was used, factoring in the company's relatively high debt-to-equity ratio (0.93) and the sensitivity of REIT valuations to prevailing interest rates.

faq.question: Why is near-term EPS growth projected to be negative?
faq.answer: The projected EPS contraction is largely a function of the financing structure. To build new capacity, DLR must issue significant amounts of equity (diluting current shares) and take on debt at higher interest rates, which temporarily depresses per-share earnings.
